title: short-circuit in INTERSECT/EXCEPT
description:
### What happens?

Hi, DuckDB developers,

Please consider such queries:
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0 INTERSECT SELECT * FROM t1;
SELECT * FROM t0 EXCEPT SELECT * FROM t1;
```
Among them, `t0` is an empty table, so the results of the above query will always be empty. Short circuit optimization can be performed (i.e. quickly returning empty). I am not saying that users intentionally write such queries, but when calculating the `INTERSECT` or `EXCEPT` among multiple tables, users may not check whether each table is empty. Therefore, I think that short circuiting the above queries is an optimization point and meaningful.

Thank you for reading my report.

Best regard,
Jinhui Lai

### To Reproduce

- Create table and insert data

```sql
.timer on
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE t1(c1 INTEGER);
INSERT INTO t1 SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 1000000);
CREATE TABLE t2(c2 INTEGER);
INSERT INTO t2 SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 10000000);
CREATE TABLE t3(c3 INTEGER);
INSERT INTO t3 SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 100000000);
```
- Positive case: these `CROSS JOIN` queries  always consume 0.001 s (i.e., short-circuit)
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0, t1;
```
```
┌───────┬───────┐
│  c0   │  c1   │
│ int32 │ int32 │
├───────┴───────┤
│    0 rows     │
└───────────────┘
Run Time (s): real 0.001 user 0.000000 sys 0.002740
```
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0, t2;
```
```
┌───────┬───────┐
│  c0   │  c2   │
│ int32 │ int32 │
├───────┴───────┤
│    0 rows     │
└───────────────┘
Run Time (s): real 0.001 user 0.000000 sys 0.002188
```
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0, t3;
```
```
┌───────┬───────┐
│  c0   │  c3   │
│ int32 │ int32 │
├───────┴───────┤
│    0 rows     │
└───────────────┘
Run Time (s): real 0.001 user 0.002316 sys 0.000737
```

- Negativa case: these `INTERSECT/EXCEPT` queries do not short-circuit. 
Moreover, duckdb process will be killed when the number of right-table's rows reaches 100 million.
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0 INTERSECT SELECT * FROM t1;
```
```
┌────────┐
│   c0   │
│ int32  │
├────────┤
│ 0 rows │
└────────┘
Run Time (s): real 0.048 user 0.106465 sys 0.015622
```
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0 INTERSECT SELECT * FROM t2;
```
```
┌────────┐
│   c0   │
│ int32  │
├────────┤
│ 0 rows │
└────────┘
Run Time (s): real 0.194 user 1.554254 sys 0.704194
```
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0 INTERSECT SELECT * FROM t3;
```
```
Killed
````

```sql
SELECT * FROM t0 EXCEPT SELECT * FROM t1;
```
```
┌────────┐
│   c0   │
│ int32  │
├────────┤
│ 0 rows │
└────────┘
Run Time (s): real 0.067 user 0.090281 sys 0.031036
```
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0 EXCEPT SELECT * FROM t2;
```
```
┌────────┐
│   c0   │
│ int32  │
├────────┤
│ 0 rows │
└────────┘
Run Time (s): real 0.193 user 1.531969 sys 0.676952
```
```sql
SELECT * FROM t0 EXCEPT SELECT * FROM t3;
```
```
Killed
```

### OS:

ubuntu 22.04

### DuckDB Version:

1.3.1

### Full Name:

Jinhui Lai

### Affiliation:

Nanchang University
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t4(c0 INTEGER, c1 VARCHAR, c2 FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE t5(c0 INTEGER, c1 BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO t5 VALUES (1, TRUE), (2, FALSE), (3, NULL);
SELECT * FROM t4 WHERE c0 IN (SELECT c0 FROM t5 WHERE c1 IS NOT NULL);
key_bug_features: Lack of short-circuit optimization for INTERSECT/EXCEPT queries
